title: astragalus bowes-lyonii
intro: Astragalus bowes-lyonii, also known as Bowles' Moly, is a perennial plant species within the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its roots and rhizomes, which contain various bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Astragalus bowes-lyonii include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce stress. Astragalus bowes-lyonii is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, where it may be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
